architecture.title: Webhook is one-way only
architecture.content: 360dialog webhook delivers messages TO our server but NEVER automatically sends replies back. We MUST explicitly POST to /v1/messages to deliver responses to users.

architecture.title: 360dialog webhook message format
architecture.content: 360dialog sends webhooks in deeply nested format: body.entry[0].changes[0].value.messages[0]. NOT body.messages[0] directly. The extractMessage() function must traverse this nesting.

architecture.title: Hybrid AI routing system
architecture.content: The bot uses a two-tier system: (1) Hardcoded pattern matching for common questions (pricing, hours, treatments) — free, instant. (2) OpenAI for complex/emotional queries — costs ~$0.002-0.003 per message. ~70% of questions are handled by hardcoded responses.

third_party.title: 360dialog sandbox endpoint
third_party.content: Sandbox API uses waba-sandbox.360dialog.io. Production uses waba.360dialog.io. Sandbox keys are ~30 uppercase characters. Sandbox can only send messages to the verified phone number.

third_party.title: Render free tier overlapping instances
third_party.content: Render free tier keeps old instances running during deploy transition. New instance starts before old one dies. Causes Telegram 409 Conflict. Fix: dropPendingUpdates: true + retry logic with delays.

third_party.title: 360dialog webhook registration via API
third_party.content: Webhook URL is configured via POST to /v1/configs/webhook with the D360-API-KEY header. NOT through a dashboard. GET to same endpoint verifies current configuration.

lessons_learned.title: Never assume webhook is two-way
lessons_learned.content: I assumed receiving a webhook meant the provider would forward our HTTP response to the user. This was wrong. 360dialog's webhook is strictly inbound. Outbound messages require a separate API call. Always check the provider's docs for the outbound endpoint.

lessons_learned.title: Verify message format with actual payload
lessons_learned.content: I assumed 360dialog's webhook format was body.messages[0] based on common patterns. The actual format is body.entry[0].changes[0].value.messages[0]. Always test with a real provider payload, never assume.

lessons_learned.title: node -c every file before deploy
lessons_learned.content: Context limits can silently truncate files during edits. A file that looks complete may be missing its last 20 lines. Running 'node -c filename.js' catches syntax errors in 0.5 seconds. This should be mandatory before every deploy.

lessons_learned.title: The deploy source matters
lessons_learned.content: The user's GitHub repo deploys from pixel-vault/backend/. Fixes in app-extract/ are invisible until synced. Always verify the deploy source path and sync fixes there before creating the ZIP.

lessons_learned.title: Circular dependencies crash silently
lessons_learned.content: bot-engine.js requires smart-router.js which requires bot-engine.js back. Node gives a vague warning: 'Accessing non-existent property inside circular dependency'. Functions silently disappear. Fix: use lazy require() inside function scope.

lessons_learned.title: Duplicate module.exports overwrites
lessons_learned.content: bot-engine.js had two module.exports blocks. The second overwrote the first. Functions exported only in the first block silently disappeared. Always grep for 'module.exports' count — should be exactly 1.

configuration.title: Render environment variables
configuration.content: Required: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, D360_API_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_ADMIN_CHAT_ID, PORT=10000, NODE_ENV=production. Optional: D360_API_URL (auto-detected). For sandbox testing: remove API_KEY to disable auth.

configuration.title: 360dialog sandbox API key format
configuration.content: Sandbox key: J6BC2J3LY2XWMKRG6ZP2S4FIA6Y2FF6C. Pattern: 32 uppercase alphanumeric characters. Auto-detected by isSandbox = key.length >= 25 && key === key.toUpperCase().

code_patterns.title: Lazy require for circular dependencies
code_patterns.content: When module A requires B and B requires A, use lazy require: const { fn } = require('./a'); inside the function that uses it, not at module top level. This breaks the circular loop at runtime.

code_patterns.title: Send WhatsApp reply pattern
code_patterns.content: After routeToAI() returns a response, always call sendWhatsAppReply(toPhone, text, messageId). Use POST to waba-sandbox.360dialog.io/v1/messages (sandbox) or waba.360dialog.io/v1/messages (production). Include D360-API-KEY header.
